courses.name: Introduction to Environmental and Climate Change Studies
courses.description: A critical introduction to the scholarly field of environmental studies, with an emphasis on society-environment entanglements. It is designed to engage with environmental and climate change issues. Possible themes include population, scarcity, institutions, commons, risks, hazards, markets, political economy, and the social construction of nature.

courses.name: People, Places and Environments
courses.description: Introduction to human geography. Examination of relationships between people, communities, society and the natural environment at local to global scales. Population change, cultural patterns, and historical, economic, political and environmental forces, including climate change,  that shape human activity and experiences from place to place.

courses.name: Environmental Justice
courses.description: Contemporary and foundational theories, practice and praxis of environmental justice in Canadian and comparative settings. Combine and communicate about aspects of the physical, built and social environments to understand how uneven conditions develop. Strategies and ideas to move towards greater equity and good environmental relationships.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing in the Environmental Studies program or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Sustainable Futures: Environmental Challenges and Solutions
courses.description: Individual and collective responses to pressing environmental problems, such as climate change. Innovative ways in which the environment can be protected and restored, taking into consideration socioeconomic, political and cultural factors. Topics include environmental lifestyles, sustainable communities, food systems, environmental design, and political activism.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing in the Environmental Studies program or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Introduction to Qualitative Research
courses.description: Introduction to the research process, from generating questions through to reporting results. Topics include intensive and extensive research approaches; the use of surveys, interviews and other data collection methods; the analysis of qualitative information; and the ethical dimensions of doing research with people and communities.
courses.prerequisites: 1.0 credit in GEOG or ENST at the 1000-level and second-year standing, or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Introduction to Quantitative Research
courses.description: Introduction to solving problems using descriptive and inferential statistical methods. Graphical and numerical tools to describe distributions. Probability, sampling and estimates, and hypothesis testing. Fundamentals of spatial statistics and analysis.

courses.name: Climate Change: Social Science Perspectives
courses.description: An introduction to climate change as a political, economic and socio-cultural phenomenon, including the political-economic and world-historical causes of anthropogenic greenhouse gas emissions; variations in impact and vulnerability; climate justice and other political movements; global mitigation and adaptation strategies; and proposals for radical systemic change.
courses.prerequisites: second-year standing or permission of the department.

courses.name: Nature, Environment and Society
courses.description: Overview of social science perspectives analyzing the relationship of nature and society.  Examination of environmental problems, responses, and potential solutions through the study of concepts, theories, and research drawn from a range of scholarly approaches to environmental and climate change studies.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing in Environmental Studies or permission of the department.

courses.name: Environmental and Natural Resources
courses.description: Exploration of complexity, dynamics, uncertainty and equity issues underpinning environmental and resource issues; review and appraisal of selected contemporary methods to assess and manage environmental and natural resources.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing in Geography or Environmental Studies or BGInS Specialization/Stream in Globalization and Environment or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Climate Justice and Action: Organizing for a Just, Equitable and Sustainable World
courses.description: Exploration of how communities and movements fight the climate crisis and build alternative futures. It combines critical theory with hands-on learning to examine the collective actions and organizing strategies through which social movements tackle the systemic factors shaping climate change and its uneven impacts.
courses.prerequisites: third-year standing or permission of the department.

courses.name: Honours Field Course
courses.description: Field research, with a focus on data collection methods, analysis and presentation of findings. Design and conduct research that links the human and biophysical environment. Topics may change from year to year.
courses.prerequisites: GEOG 2005 / ENST 2005 and GEOG 2006 / ENST 2006 , third-year Honours standing in Environmental Studies, Geomatics, or Geography, or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Environmental and Climate Change Studies Seminar
courses.description: An advanced seminar designed to provide a capstone experience that builds upon and applies the analytical skills and interdisciplinary knowledge acquired in the Environmental Studies program. Topics vary year to year and by course section (see departmental website).
courses.prerequisites: Registration is restricted to students eligible for fourth-year standing in the B.A. (Environmental Studies) Honours program.

courses.name: Environmental Studies Practicum I
courses.description: Students apply their knowledge and research skills and gain experience through field placements in government, the private sector, non-government organisations and with community organisations in the environmental field.
courses.prerequisites: registration is restricted to students eligible for fourth-year standing in the B.A. (Environmental Studies) Honours program, and permission of the Environmental Studies Co-ordinator.

courses.name: Environmental Studies Practicum II
courses.description: Students apply their knowledge and research skills and gain experience through field placements in government, the private sector, non-government organisations and with community organisations in the environmental field.
courses.prerequisites: restricted to students in the fourth year of the Environmental Studies Honours program, and permission of the Environmental Studies Co-ordinator.

courses.name: Environmental Impact Assessment
courses.description: Principles, scope and purpose of environmental impact assessment, from conceptual and methodological points of view; range of environmental issues, with emphasis on Canadian case studies.
courses.prerequisites: GEOG 3022 or ENST 3022 , and fourth-year Honours standing in Geography or Environmental Studies or Environmental Science, or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Directed Studies in Environmental Studies
courses.description: Students pursue their interest in a selected theme in environmental studies on a tutorial basis with a faculty member.
courses.prerequisites: permission of the Department.

courses.name: Environmental Policy Analysis
courses.description: Critical examination of the creation, implementation and effectiveness of government policies related to environmental issues. Emphasis on perspectives, actors, institutions and social and economic relationships affecting policy responses to these issues, and on tools for analyzing the implications of specific policy choices.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing in Environmental Studies, Geography, or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Special Topics in Geography and Environmental Studies
courses.description: Selected topics in geography and/or environmental studies.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing in the Department or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Seminar in People, Resources, and Environmental Change
courses.description: A selected topic or field of inquiry concerning natural resource use and environmental change.
courses.prerequisites: GEOG 3022 or ENST 3022 and fourth-year Honours standing in Geography or Environmental Studies or BGInS Specialization in Globalization and Environment, or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Environmental and Geographic Education
courses.description: Selected theoretical and applied issues concerning environmental and geographic education.
courses.prerequisites: Third-year honours standing in Geography or Environmental Studies, or permission of the Department.

courses.name: Field Studies
courses.description: Field observation and methodology in a selected region, special topic or contemporary problem; on an individual or group basis.
courses.prerequisites: third-year Honours standing and permission of the Department.

courses.name: Community-Engaged Research
courses.description: Working in partnership with local organizations, students apply their geographical knowledge to conduct community-engaged research. Student projects will generate outputs for community partners. Research topics vary year to year.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year standing, or permission of the department.

courses.name: Honours Research Project
courses.description: An independent investigation into a select aspect of environmental studies, supervised by a faculty member. Possible outcomes might include: workshops, audio-visual productions, lay publications, and field projects accompanied by an essay demonstrating the student's capacity to critically reflect on the research project.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing in Environmental Studies, a minimum CGPA of 9.00 in the major or permission of the Department, and an approved research topic and adviser.

courses.name: Honours Research Essay
courses.description: Interdisciplinary research essay on an environmental issue, carried out in consultation with a faculty supervisor. The student must consult with the undergraduate student advisor in selecting a project and a supervisor.
courses.prerequisites: fourth-year Honours standing in Environmental Studies, a minimum CGPA of 9.00 in the major or permission of the Department, and an approved research topic and adviser.

free_electives.degree: Environmental and Climate Change Studies  B.A. Honours (20.0 credits)
